methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Theion is a Series A-stage climate and energy company founded by Ulrich Ehmes that has raised €25M+ to date. The valuation analysis relies on a peer-median methodology applied to a comparable universe of 3 same-sector, same-stage private companies: Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M). The broader comp set includes 8 private firms and 1 public benchmark (LNZA).

The peer-median method yields a central valuation of $150M, with constituent peers spanning a $110M–$200M range. This represents the most directly applicable triangulation given stage and sector alignment. The median peer multiple reflects current market pricing for early-stage climate-tech capital deployment and technology-to-commercialization risk. No other valuation methods were applied given data constraints; a single-method conclusion carries inherent limitation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $128M–$172M, centered on $150M. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to peer burn rates, competitive positioning within the thermal energy storage or long-duration battery segments Theion likely occupies, and timing to meaningful revenue inflection. The narrow peer set (n=3) introduces concentration risk; broader sampling across the 8-peer private universe and public comparables would strengthen conviction. For a Series A entering capital-intensive scaling phases, unit economics and path-to-profitability warrant particular scrutiny.
